TextENGNew: The free study period during class was a convenient time to consider my current situation.
I closed my eyes lightly and resolved to think through the absurd predicament I found myself in.
First... what I must not forget... was just how dangerous my situation truly was.
I had become someone who knew things they didn't want me to know.
changeReason: Old translation 'fallen out of favor' misses the Japanese meaning that Keiichi has become someone who knows things they don't want known (好ましくないことを知る人間). New translation captures this key plot point.

TextENGNew: After repeated contact with Ooishi-san, it appeared that I was closing in on the truth.
It was reasonable to assume yesterday's ohagi had been a warning.
changeReason: Food item corrected from 'mochi' to 'ohagi' (おはぎ); the Japanese text specifically says おはぎ. Also restructured from 'warning was an indicator' to 'ohagi was a warning'.

TextENGNew: ......No, ...calling it a 'warning' was itself a typically timid way of putting it.
To them, those ohagi served no purpose beyond intimidating me and buying time.
...Buying time......until they could 'erase' me by a more thorough method.
Even if I caved to their threats, the fact remained: I was someone who knew too much.
Just then, the cool touch of the desk reminded me of Satoshi Houjou -- the boy who had sat here until last year before he vanished.
Had he been... in the same position as me?
Did he learn things he shouldn't have... and get erased...?
......Damn...!@k I wouldn't... let them get rid of me that easily...... absolutely not...!!
But... was someone really trying to kill me...?
These contradictory feelings had been churning inside me for a while -- suspecting Rena and the others while simultaneously wanting to defend them.
changeReason: New translation references different characters/places: Rena.

TextENGNew: Even having witnessed all that suspicious behavior and those strange words, sitting here in the morning sunlight, it all felt like it could be a lie......@k No -- I was trying to convince myself it was.
Was I doubting my friends?
Shielding them?
Was my life being targeted?
changeReason: JPN is a question (命を狙われている？) but old renders it as a statement ('My life was in danger...'); new correctly uses question form. Also 狙われている (being targeted) is more specific than 'in danger.'

TextENGNew: Or wasn't it?
I was debating entirely the wrong point.
Given the reality of my situation... that debate should have been settled long ago...
Hey......was someone really... trying to kill me...?@k Rena and the others......
Another part of me lashed out at my indecisive thoughts.
Are you stupid, Maebara Keiichi?! The answer is already obvious!!
B-But... maybe... the sewing needle really was just a coincidence, an accident...?
How does a sewing needle accidentally end up in ohagi?! Enough with the wishful thinking!!
changeReason: Food item corrected from 'mochi' to 'ohagi', and 'benefit of the doubt can only go so far' changed to 'enough with the wishful thinking' (お人好しも大概にしろ).

TextENGNew: Sure, Rena and Mion have both... acted and spoken suspiciously... but maybe it's all just a misunderstanding...
A misunderstanding of what?!@k It wasn't just suspicious... it was clearly abnormal!!
Rena only called me out for lying... and Mion only asked what I had for lunch...
That same Rena was eavesdropping outside your room for a full hour?!
Th-That was probably... she was just waiting for my phone call to end...
For a whole hour, standing outside your door?! And then just going home without saying a word -- is that normal?!
Ooishi-san told you, didn't he?!@k What Rena did at her previous school!!
B-But... she told the doctor at the hospital it was Oyashiro-sama's fault...
``Give it a rest, Maebara Keiichi. Do you even realize your life is in danger?!``
changeReason: Name order changed to Japanese order (Maebara Keiichi). Also 'they're out to kill you' (specific threat) changed to 'your life is in danger' (general danger).

TextENGNew: Those were words I'd unconsciously spoken aloud.
Shocked by my own bluntness... I sat stunned for a moment, then quickly looked around to see if anyone had heard.
......That soliloquy... cut far too close to the truth.
Even while sensing murderous intent from Rena and the others... some part of me kept denying it.
At this point... that kind of hesitation could be fatal.@k ...I understood that.
But... I was just an ordinary schoolboy... a perfectly normal guy with a perfectly normal upbringing!
Friends I'd been laughing and having fun with just last Sunday... could I really just suddenly believe they wanted me dead...?! Come on...?
``Stop being soft, Keiichi!!!!``
This time I did it consciously, clenching my gut and keeping it to a whisper only I could hear.
There was one thing I now understood.
I was... too soft.
I didn't grasp how truly dangerous Rena and the others were.@k No -- I wasn't even trying to grasp it.
I'd been complacent, taking Ooishi-san's consideration for granted and refusing to hear the heart of the matter.
changeReason: Characterization changed: old says 'I was too soft for not listening' while new says 'I'd been complacent, taking Ooishi-san's consideration for granted'. The new version more accurately reflects 甘え (complacency/taking advantage of kindness).

TextENGNew: By feigning dismay, I'd stopped listening.@k I hadn't tried to understand.@k I'd been running away.
The delusion that 'since I can't comprehend it, there's no murderous intent' -- I had to abandon that kind of naive thinking...!
changeReason: Restructured from question form ('nobody had any notion to kill me?') to quoted delusion format ('the delusion that since I can't comprehend it, there's no murderous intent'). New version is clearer and closer to the Japanese structure.

TextENGNew: Just as I steeled myself to that resolve, the final bell rang.
...Already?
The day was already over.
I had no memory of eating lunch, no memory of attending any class. Nothing remained.
My friends were pushing their desks together, setting up for the after-school club.
The old me would have eagerly thrown myself into that circle...
``My my, Keiichi-san?@k@vS01/04/120400058. How rude of you to be packing up to leave~!``
Satoko's tone was so perfectly normal... it made my chest ache.
``Huh? Keiichi-kun, is today no good, I wonder? ...I wonder?@k@vS01/02/120200194. Today we were finally going to win together... hau...``
Rena's crestfallen expression -- she'd been looking forward to it -- was hard to bear.
Hey, Keiichi... what if Ooishi-san was actually the villain here, feeding me lies to isolate me from everyone...?
*SLAP!!*
To drive out that feeble thought, I slapped my own cheek with my palm.
``K-Keiichi-kun...?``
Ahh... was Rena truly trying to kill me...??
I wanted someone to tell me it was all a lie.@k Even if it were itself a lie, I wanted to hear someone say it.
......Guh, another soft-hearted thought...!!
Why was I... so hopelessly naive?!
``A-Are you okay...? Does your head hurt?``
It seemed that my private anguish looked like a headache to Rena.
changeReason: New translation adds specific detail that Keiichi's anguish 'looked like a headache to Rena', which is implied by the Japanese context but absent from the original.

TextENGNew: ``You look pale.@k@vS01/02/120200198. Can you make it home alone?@k@vS01/02/120200199. ...Should I walk you home, I wonder? ...I wonder?``
``No... it's fine. ...Sorry.@k@vS01/01/120100375. I can get home by myself.@k@vS01/01/120100376. Go ahead and do club activities without me.``
When she heard I wasn't joining club, Mion puffed out her cheeks in displeasure.
``Kei-chan was so fired up for a rematch, so we even picked that mystery game from last time and everything.``
``Keiichi-san, don't you have the burning passion for a rematch?@k@vS01/04/120400060. How pathetic~!``
I wasn't going to rise to some cheap provocation.
Without so much as a retort, I grabbed my bag and started to stand.
Then someone gently placed a hand on my head.
``......Keiichi doesn't look well.@k@vS01/05/120500029. ......Poor, poor thing.``
It was Rika-chan.
Stretching up on her tiptoes as far as she could, she patted my head softly with her small hand...
It felt so comforting... and that only made it harder.
With only those words, I hurried out of the classroom.
They seemed to be saying something as they watched me go, but their words didn't reach my ears.
I headed straight for the entrance hall, retrieved my shoes, put them on, and pressed forward. Forward.
Empty your mind......Maebara Keiichi...!
They were... for whatever incomprehensible reason... after my life!!
They were plotting something sinister, watching my every move like hawks!!
But... I couldn't hate them...!!
Because... they were my friends, weren't they?!!
Part of me cursed my own softness, while another part of me wondered -- by cursing it, had I already lost something I should never have let go?
It felt as though my very identity was being torn apart, body and all.
If this was what Oyashiro-sama's curse was like...@k ...then it was just... too cruel.
Hey, Oyashiro-sama... I was wrong for not believing in your curse...
But I believe now.
I truly believe.
Your curse is real...
So please, seriously......give me a break............@k I'm begging you............come on...
For once, dinner was tasteless.
No flavor, no aroma.
...Even the steam rising from the miso soup, which would normally whet my appetite, registered as nothing more than plain vapor.
changeReason: Old translation says miso soup 'tasted like boiled water' (about taste), but Japanese 湯気/水蒸気 refers to steam/vapor. New translation correctly describes the steam rather than taste.

TextENGNew: Dad was at the dinner table with us tonight.
That was a rarity in this household.
Once he got absorbed in his work, eating and sleeping happened on his own terms.@k He was the kind of father who paid no attention to the clock.
When Dad actually sat down for dinner, it meant either he'd reached a good stopping point in his work, or he was in a slump. One or the other.
``Honestly, the man just doesn't understand what drives an artist.``
``The partnership deal was only a verbal agreement, wasn't it?@k@vS01/00/122700047. If you two really don't get along, why not just cut ties cleanly?``
``It's an industry built on mutual help, so I get the give-and-take... but like this, I can't work...``
Judging from the conversation between Dad and Mom, it wasn't exactly a pleasant exchange.
......Naturally, the already unappetizing meal became even harder to stomach.
Watching my parents' back-and-forth, I absentmindedly continued the line of thinking I'd been pursuing all day.
...My close friends -- no, the people I'd thought were friends -- could no longer be trusted. I was desperately short on allies.
changeReason: New translation uses different key action verbs, changing the described events or emotions.

TextENGNew: There wasn't a single person I could rely on, someone trustworthy I could turn to in a crisis.
...Even just one ally would have been immeasurably reassuring in this hopeless situation...
I set down my chopsticks and looked up at my parents, still deep in their work talk.
Telling my parents everything was the very first option I'd considered.
Now that I couldn't trust a single person in Hinamizawa one hundred percent, the only people I could trust were my own parents.
But... if I told them everything that had happened up to now, would they actually understand?
Take Rena, for example.
Since I transferred here, she'd looked after me devotedly, came to walk me to school every morning, and even brought over home-cooked food from time to time.
changeReason: New translation adds 'since I transferred here' and 'came to walk me to school every morning' (毎朝迎えに来てくれ), details present in Japanese but omitted in original.

TextENGNew: How could I possibly explain... that she was trying to kill me??
No matter how I framed it, getting anyone to accept that would be incredibly difficult...
My somewhat eccentric father would never comprehend it, and my high-strung mother would probably drag me straight to a psychiatrist.
It was sad, but... that was the extent of the trust I'd built with them.
Even if they did believe me... what could they actually do?
changeReason: Changed 'come to understand' to 'believe me' — different threshold (intellectual comprehension vs belief/trust).

TextENGNew: They couldn't uncover the truth, and they couldn't protect my life.
...No, rather -- by telling them these 'unnecessary things,' I'd be putting my parents in danger too...!
Considering how often past victims had been couples... it was no laughing matter.
It was entirely possible for the three-person Maebara family to simply vanish from Hinamizawa -- claimed by an 'accident' or evaporated without a trace......
The key point here was that knowing 'unnecessary things' put you in danger.
What was chilling was... how had they found out that I 'knew'...?
But flipping it around, couldn't I also argue that 'as long as they didn't know,' my parents wouldn't be harmed...?
At least that was how it worked in my case.@k Everything went wrong after I 'found out.'
...In other words, this was the logic:
As long as my parents remained ignorant, nothing would happen to them.
And as long as those oblivious parents remained in this house, this house would be a safe zone from them.
......I knew this was speculation built upon conjecture stacked upon conjecture.
Wanting this house to be a safe haven... was nothing more than my own cowardice seeking comfort.
...I had to understand that it was merely safer than being outside... not absolutely safe.
My parents couldn't be counted on... no -- I couldn't drag my parents into this. That much was clear.
Then the only person who could be my ally... was Ooishi-san. No one else.
Ooishi-san was the only person who understood my current situation.
He might not have cared much about keeping me safe, but his passion for solving the case was beyond question.
......It was a little galling.
...Ooishi-san was the root cause of this entire mess, and yet to get out of it, I had to rely on him again......
In other words... everything was proceeding exactly according to his plan.
I was chum scattered across the sea -- my job was to bob appetizingly among the waves.@k ...And when the fish swarmed around me, Ooishi-san would net them all in one sweep!
It was a little frustrating, but... I couldn't think of a better option.
So then... what was I supposed to do?!
...The cardinal rule of fishing was patience.
Just lie low and wait, endlessly, until the fish takes the bait...
But I wasn't merely bait.
I could make every effort to resist before being devoured.
When they made their move, I'd have to somehow dodge by a hair's breadth and hand things off to Ooishi-san.
Naturally, it wouldn't be easy.@k ...The timing for bringing Ooishi-san in would be tricky.
Ooishi-san was in the city, not Hinamizawa.@k So even if I called him in an emergency, it would take at least thirty minutes for him to arrive.@k I'd have to survive those thirty minutes somehow.
For instance... if we agreed on a predetermined meeting point for emergencies.@k ...I could hide there until Ooishi-san arrived.
``...Alright......I'm starting to... see a way forward...``
I was still trapped in a darkness of paranoia, that was certain. But knowing what direction to take from here was more reassuring than I'd imagined.
changeReason: Changed from 'being chased around in the dark by boogiemen' (active pursuit metaphor) to 'trapped in a darkness of paranoia' (passive state). Different characterization of the threat.

TextENGNew: Right.
In case of emergency, it might be wise to have some kind of portable weapon ready.
changeReason: Changed 'concealed weapon' to 'portable weapon'. Japanese 携帯できる武器 means 'portable weapon', not necessarily concealed.

TextENGNew: ......The obvious choice would be a folding knife.
changeReason: Changed 'switchblade' to 'folding knife'. Japanese 折り畳みナイフ literally means folding knife, not a switchblade (which has a specific mechanism).

TextENGNew: But it would be unreliable in an actual fight.
And the fact that it was socially recognized as a weapon was also a problem.
When it came down to it... a long weapon like a bat would be far more advantageous.
I remembered there was a metal bat at school.@k ...That would be reassuring to have in a crisis.
...If I pretended to be practicing my swing, carrying it around wouldn't seem suspicious.
...I'd go to school early tomorrow and secure it.
Simply possessing a weapon might be enough to deter them from acting.
And one more thing... insurance.
A note, a written message -- anything. I should write down what was happening, diary-style.
Even if I 'vanished,' the diary would remain.
...Ooishi-san could use my diary to avenge me.
Leaving my parents absorbed in their work talk, I went back up to my room.
I wasted no time tearing a page from my notebook and sitting down at my desk.
......A diary -- I hadn't written one since summer homework in elementary school.
In the worst case, the police would use my diary as evidence.@k ...So I should keep it concise, stating only the facts.
changeReason: Changed 'as a lead' to 'as evidence' (手がかり can mean both, but changes the evidentiary framing). Also 'off chance' changed to 'worst case'.

TextENGNew: How should I start......
I began writing, jotting things down as they came to mind.
'I, Maebara Keiichi, am being targeted for my life.'
I let out a bitter laugh. I never dreamed I'd be the one writing a line that belonged in a mystery novel...
'I do not know why, or by whom, my life is being targeted.'
I suspected Rena and the others, but I had no proof.@k ......So I couldn't write any more than that.
The passage was so absurdly mystery-novel-esque that I couldn't help but laugh at myself.
Would the police actually be able to glean anything from this cryptic message?
I could only pray they would.
...Though what I prayed for most was that this diary would never need to serve its purpose...@k I let out another bitter laugh.
It felt too sparse, so I added one more line off the top of my head.
'The only thing I do know is that it is connected to the curse of Oyashiro-sama.'
Was that... too much to write...?
......I probably shouldn't write any more.@k ......Any more than this and it would start reading like a paranoid rant.
To convey that the writer was of sound mind, I shouldn't put down anything beyond this for now.
......I could always add more as new facts came to light.
I folded the note and considered where to hide it.
......If I hid it somewhere obvious, there was a risk they'd find it in the worst-case scenario.
But if I hid it somewhere too obscure, there was the danger no one would ever find it...
After deliberating, I took the clock off my wall, taped the folded note to the back, and hung it back up.@k Then I put the clock back in its place.
...Good.@k You'd never guess anything was hidden behind it.
All that remained was to set things up so my parents would find this note 'if anything happened to me.'
I checked it from several different angles, and once satisfied, headed back downstairs.
My parents were still going on about work, showing no signs of stopping.
It didn't look like the conversation would end anytime soon, so I decided to cut in.
``Hey, sorry to interrupt!!@k@vS01/01/120100382. ......There's something I need you to hear.``
I never started conversations this way, so perhaps it startled them -- both of my parents stopped talking immediately and turned to face me.
``What is it, Keiichi?``
``I have a favor to ask. Please listen.@k@vS01/01/120100384. ......This is hypothetical, okay?``
``Keiichi, if it's not urgent, save it for later.@k@vS01/00/122600019. Your father and I are in the middle of something important right now.``
changeReason: Changed 'Mommy and Daddy' to 'Your father and I' — different speech register; the parent speaks more formally/maturely in the new version.

TextENGNew: I doubted their conversation was more urgent than mine. I pressed on anyway.
``This is hypothetical, okay?@k@vS01/01/120100386. If I die...``
Both of my parents' eyes went wide.
``If I die...@k@vS01/01/120100388. ......I want you to put the clock from my room into the coffin with me.``
If they did that, they'd notice it. The note with my diary.
changeReason: Old translates メモ (note/memo) as 'memoir' — different document type, though context helps. New correctly says 'note with my diary.'

TextENGNew: My parents remained frozen, eyes still wide.
......I couldn't blame them.
``That clock's my favorite -- I made it in shop class.@k@vS01/01/120100390. Please.``
``...What's going on?@k@vS01/00/122700050. Keiichi.@k@vS01/00/122700051. ......Did something happen?``
Mom finally managed to speak, her face full of concern.
changeReason: Changed 'questioning gaze' (怪訝) to 'face full of concern'. 怪訝 means puzzled/suspicious, not concerned. The old translation is actually closer to the Japanese.

TextENGNew: If your son suddenly started saying things like this, that would be the natural reaction.
I felt bad for worrying them, but right now all I needed was for them to be aware of the 'clock' in my room.
The room had fallen into an awkward, oppressive silence, so I decided to retreat upstairs.
``I want to get to school early tomorrow, so I'm going to sleep.@k@vS01/01/120100392. Good night.``
Leaving only those words behind, I left the living room.
Tomorrow I'd go to school early and secure the bat.
Today should be the last day I walked to school with Rena.
As I climbed the stairs, Mom called my name, but I pretended not to hear.
There was nothing I could consult my parents about.
Talking to them about it... would only increase the danger.
A battle no one else could fight for me -- my solitary war -- was about to begin.
I would not be killed.@k ...Not while I still didn't even know why... absolutely not.
